text: fat is 65.2g. the fat quality is doing a lot of the work here. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
carbs is 13.7g. sugar, starch, and glycemic index are doing most of the work. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.
protein is 15.2g. essential amino acids score is where things start to fall off. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 4% DV, vitamin b12 at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 38% DV, zinc at 21% DV, iron at 16% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side. contains useful polyphenols because adds plant-compound value beyond the core table. has strong healthy-fat branding appeal because a practical content advantage because viewers already associate walnuts with healthy fats. can be very satiating because dense fats and texture can help fullness in practice.

the drawbacks next. easy to overeat calories quickly because energy density adds up fast. contains some anti-nutrients because phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. can go rancid if stored badly because a practical downside versus some sturdier staples.
S tier. Best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
